channel.title: Pages on Dimitri Fontaine
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on Dimitri Fontaine

item.title: Mastering PostgreSQL in Application Development
item.description: I am writing a book to share my knowledge about how to best use PostgreSQL as an application developer. If you’ve evered ask yourself how much should I have my database do for me? this book is for you. If you’re thinking that using an ORM might not always be the best option out there, if sometimes you wish SQL was easier to maintain than those concatenated strings with conditionals, then this book can’t wait for you!

item.title: About Dimitri Fontaine
item.description:
Dimitri, picture by Oleg Bartunov

Hi! My name is Dimitri. I have been using and contributing to Open Source Software for the best part of the last twenty years — as a developer, maintainer, packager, release manager, software and database architect, and sometimes devops. In the same time frame I started several companies with a strong Open Source business model, and held manager positions at times too, up to the Executive level.

item.title: Emacs Lisp
item.description: I have lived in Emacs for two decades. These are the Emacs Lisp packages I wrote and still maintain — and there is plenty more Emacs writing in the Emacs archive.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Open-source tools I build and maintain — mostly about getting data in and out of PostgreSQL and keeping it highly available. The full graveyard lives on my GitHub profile.
